payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields should capture usage, identity, cost, and latency in a consistent way. Token counts should include input or prompt tokens, output or completion tokens, and total tokens; if available, also include cached, reasoning, or tool-related token breakdowns separately. Provider and model IDs should record the exact provider name and model identifier used for the run, such as the API vendor and model version or deployment ID, so results are reproducible. Estimated price should be computed from the recorded token counts and the applicable provider pricing schedule, usually split into input cost, output cost, and total estimated cost, with currency and pricing timestamp or version when possible. Latency fields should report response-time distributions across evaluated requests: p50 is the median latency, p90 is the latency below which 90% of requests completed, and p95 is the latency below which 95% of requests completed. Latency should specify units, typically milliseconds, and define whether it measures end-to-end wall time, provider API time, streaming first-token latency, or full completion latency.

entity_resolution.reason: The request asks for an explanation of evaluation accounting fields, not for storing or updating durable personal memory.

details: Explained latency percentiles p50, p90, and p95.
